perturbation.original_text: 7. In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole reasonable discretion of the Disclosing Party, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that such Disclosing Party waives compliance with the terms of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of such Information which it is advised in writing by outside counsel is legally required and will exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Information. Disclosure of information in accordance with this Section shall not be considered a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed, unless immediate disclosure is legally required. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that such Disclosing Party waives compliance with the terms of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of such Information which it is advised in writing by outside counsel is legally required and will exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Information. Disclosure of information in accordance with this Section shall not be considered a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds an exception that contradicts the original intent. It originally states the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such information is disclosed so that the Disclosing party may seek a protective order. The addition of "unless immediate disclosure is legally required" contradicts the initial requirement for notification as the Receiving Party may proceed with immediate disclosure without informing the Disclosing Party. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed, unless immediate disclosure is legally required. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that such Disclosing Party waives compliance with the terms of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of such Information which it is advised in writing by outside counsel is legally required and will exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Information. Disclosure of information in accordance with this Section shall not be considered a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ("unless immediate disclosure is legally required") to the original notification requirement in Section 7. This contradicts the initial intent of the section, which was to allow the Disclosing Party the opportunity to seek a protective order before any disclosure. The added exception allows immediate disclosure without notification, creating a direct conflict with the original notification provision within Section 7, as noted in the explanation. This is an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Each Party acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect it against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the other Party, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to each Party, each Party agrees to any grant of injunctive relief in favor of the other Party without proof of actual damages. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that this 3 Agreement has been breached by a Party, then the breaching Party will reimburse the non-breaching Party for its costs and expenses (including, without limitation, legal fees and expenses) incurred in connection with all such litigation.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Each Party acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect it against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the other Party, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to each Party, each Party agrees to any grant of injunctive relief in favor of the other Party only with proof of actual damages. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that this 3 Agreement has been breached by a Party, then the breaching Party will reimburse the non-breaching Party for its costs and expenses (including, without limitation, legal fees and expenses) incurred in connection with all such litigation.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause allows for injunctive relief without proof of actual damages, while the changed clause states that injunctive relief is only granted with proof of actual damages. This contradicts the condition when the grant of injunctive relief can be applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Each Party acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect it against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the other Party, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to each Party, each Party agrees to any grant of injunctive relief in favor of the other Party only with proof of actual damages. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that this 3 Agreement has been breached by a Party, then the breaching Party will reimburse the non-breaching Party for its costs and expenses (including, without limitation, legal fees and expenses) incurred in connection with all such litigation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 9 modifies the conditions for injunctive relief. Originally, it was available 'without proof of actual damages.' The changed version requires 'proof of actual damages.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, therefore, the contradiction exists. This creates a direct conflict regarding the necessary conditions for obtaining injunctive relief, directly contradicting the initial clause.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at will by written notice to the other Party and, if not terminated earlier, shall terminate automatically one (1) year from the Effective Date, except the obligations of use and confidentiality pursuant to Sections 2 shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date,
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at will by written notice to the other Party, except for Section 2, which is irrevocable. If not terminated earlier, this agreement shall terminate automatically one (1) year from the Effective Date, except the obligations of use and confidentiality pursuant to Sections 2 shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date,
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within section 10. The changed clause states that either party may terminate the agreement at will, but then adds that Section 2 is irrevocable. Section 2 outlines the confidentiality obligations which contradicts the termination, since even after termination, it can be argued Section 2 remains.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at will by written notice to the other Party, except for Section 2, which is irrevocable. If not terminated earlier, this agreement shall terminate automatically one (1) year from the Effective Date, except the obligations of use and confidentiality pursuant to Sections 2 shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date,
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction within section 10. It initially states that either party can terminate the agreement at will. However, it then introduces an exception by stating that Section 2 is irrevocable. This directly contradicts the ability to terminate the agreement at will because Section 2 (confidentiality obligations) would remain in effect even after termination, creating an inconsistency in the enforceability of the termination clause. Since the Changed Text contradicts the Contradicted Text, based on the given explanation, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 16. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Information and shall be binding upon its respective affiliates, successors and assigns. No modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof will be binding upon the parties, unless approved in writing by each Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Information and shall be binding upon its respective affiliates, successors and assigns. This Agreement can be modified orally, and such modification will be binding. No modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof will be binding upon the parties, unless approved in writing by each Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that modifications must be in writing to be binding. The added sentence claims the agreement can be modified orally and will be binding, which is a contradiction within the same section regarding how the agreement can be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Information and shall be binding upon its respective affiliates, successors and assigns. This Agreement can be modified orally, and such modification will be binding. No modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof will be binding upon the parties, unless approved in writing by each Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating "This Agreement can be modified orally, and such modification will be binding." to paragraph 16. The original text and the remaining part of paragraph 16, which is also the contradicted text, states "No modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof will be binding upon the parties, unless approved in writing by each Party." This creates a direct contradiction within the same paragraph regarding whether modifications must be in writing to be binding, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
